## [00:00 - 01:50]

Okay, so you've done all the grunt work, you've found the prospect, you've found the business owner and you've recorded a loom video. Now you need to make sure that all that time isn't wasted. You've personalized all of the steps so far and you want to make sure this last step of finding the email and sending the email is also personalized. You don't want to have just a template and email that people read and then they don't even end up watching that loom video that you spent so much time creating. So here's where you use a tool called Hunter. Hunter is a Google Chrome extension and basically what it does is it scrapes websites and you're able to find emails on that website associated with the website. So you might find the business owner just by searching that way. You can also find it just on the website on the Contact Us page. Another alternative if you can't find it on either of those is through LinkedIn. LinkedIn often someone will have their business email in their profile so you might be able to just find it there. Especially if you've added that person as a contact already. So beyond that you actually need to craft this email and you need to make sure it's something that isn't spammy. It's something that acknowledges the fact that you've probably never contacted this person before and then you want to make sure that you state the reason you're actually sending the email. All in doing so you want to make sure this is personalized and it's not something that is just a very cookie cutter response. You want to make sure you drop as many personalizations as you possibly can and throughout this loom video and throughout the process of recording and doing your analysis on the account and the analysis on kind of what they're currently advertising you can pull some things there and then just enter those into your email so it doesn't even take any additional time. So we'll give you an example as well in the next video but after that you just need to send away.
